title: EU Regulation 2016/425 on Personal Protective Equipment - Respiratory Protection
title_zh: 欧盟法规2016/425个人防护设备法规 - 呼吸防护
summary: Regulation laying down harmonised standards for respiratory protective devices placed on the EU market, covering filtering facepieces, half masks, and full-face respirators.
summary_zh: 为在欧盟市场投放的呼吸防护设备制定协调标准的法规，涵盖过滤式面罩、半面罩和全面罩呼吸器。

title: EN 149:2001+A1:2009 - Respiratory protective devices - Filtering half masks
title_zh: EN 149:2001+A1:2009 - 呼吸防护装置 - 过滤式半面罩
summary: European standard specifying minimum requirements for filtering half masks used as respiratory protective devices, specifically against particles.
summary_zh: 欧洲标准，规定了用作呼吸防护设备的过滤式半面罩的最低要求，特别是针对颗粒物。
full_text:
## 1 Scope
This European Standard specifies minimum requirements for filtering half masks used as respiratory protective devices, specifically against particles, and the required laboratory and practical performance tests to assess masks' compliance.

This standard is not applicable to filtering half masks used in hypoxic atmospheres (oxygen content less than 17% by volume) or against biological agents at concentrations sufficient to endanger life.

## 2 Normative references
The following referenced documents are indispensable for the application of this document:
- EN 132:1998, Respiratory protective devices - Definitions of terms and pictograms
- EN 134:2002, Respiratory protective devices - Nomenclature of components
- EN 143:2000, Respiratory protective devices - Particle filters - Requirements, testing, marking
- EN 13274-1:2000, Respiratory protective devices - Methods of test - Part 1: Determination of inward leakage

## 3 Terms and definitions
For the purposes of this European Standard, the following terms and definitions apply:

### 3.1 filtering half mask
A facepiece in which the main filter(s) form an inseparable part of the device.

### 3.2 particle filter
A device which removes particulate matter from the inspired air.

### 3.3 total inward leakage (TIL)
The amount of a specific challenge agent which has leaked into the respiratory protective device, taking into account both face seal leakage and filter penetration.

### 3.4 penetration
The amount of a specific challenge agent which has passed through the filter medium.

### 3.5 FFP1, FFP2, FFP3
Classification of filtering half masks based on their filtering efficiency and maximum total inward leakage.

## 4 Classification
Filtering half masks shall be classified according to their filtering efficiency and maximum total inward leakage as follows:

### 4.1 FFP1
- Filtering efficiency: at least 80%
- Maximum total inward leakage: 22%
- Suitable for protection against non-toxic solid and liquid aerosols

### 4.2 FFP2
- Filtering efficiency: at least 94%
- Maximum total inward leakage: 8%
- Suitable for protection against moderate toxicity solid and liquid aerosols

### 4.3 FFP3
- Filtering efficiency: at least 99%
- Maximum total inward leakage: 2%
- Suitable for protection against high toxicity solid and liquid aerosols

## 5 Requirements

### 5.1 Materials
Materials used in the construction of the filtering half mask shall be suitable for the intended use and shall not pose a risk to the wearer.

### 5.2 Design
The filtering half mask shall be designed to fit the range of face sizes for which it is intended.

### 5.3 Performance requirements
#### 5.3.1 Particle filtering efficiency
The particle filtering efficiency shall meet the requirements specified in clause 4 for the relevant class.

#### 5.3.2 Total inward leakage (TIL)
The total inward leakage shall not exceed the values specified in clause 4 for the relevant class when tested in accordance with EN 13274-1.

#### 5.3.3 Breathing resistance
The breathing resistance shall not exceed the following values:
- FFP1: Inhalation resistance not exceeding 3.0 mbar at 30 l/min; Exhalation resistance not exceeding 3.0 mbar at 160 l/min
- FFP2: Inhalation resistance not exceeding 3.0 mbar at 30 l/min; Exhalation resistance not exceeding 3.0 mbar at 160 l/min
- FFP3: Inhalation resistance not exceeding 3.0 mbar at 30 l/min; Exhalation resistance not exceeding 3.0 mbar at 160 l/min

#### 5.3.4 Clogging resistance (optional)
Where claimed by the manufacturer, the filtering half mask shall be tested for clogging resistance using dolomite dust.

### 5.4 Marking
Each filtering half mask shall be marked with:
(a) the manufacturer's name or identifying mark;
(b) the name and/or model number;
(c) the classification (FFP1, FFP2, or FFP3);
(d) the year of manufacture or batch number;
(e) the number of this European Standard (EN 149:2001+A1:2009);
(f) the letters 'R' or 'NR' indicating re-usable or non-reusable.

## 6 Testing

### 6.1 Test conditions
All tests shall be carried out at a temperature of (23 ± 2)°C and a relative humidity of (50 ± 5)%, unless otherwise specified.

### 6.2 Particle filtering efficiency test
The particle filtering efficiency shall be determined using a sodium chloride test aerosol or a paraffin oil test aerosol.

### 6.3 Total inward leakage test
The total inward leakage shall be determined in accordance with EN 13274-1 using human test subjects.

### 6.4 Breathing resistance test
The breathing resistance shall be measured using a breathing machine or human test subjects.

## 7 Information to be supplied by the manufacturer

### 7.1 Instructions for use
The manufacturer shall provide instructions for use with each filtering half mask, containing at least the following information:
(a) identification of the product;
(b) intended use and limitations;
(c) fitting instructions;
(d) method of use;
(e) method of checking the fit (fit check);
(f) cleaning and disinfection instructions (for re-usable devices);
(g) storage conditions;
(h) warnings and precautions;
(i) expiry date or shelf life.

### 7.2 Packaging
The packaging shall be marked with:
(a) the manufacturer's name and address;
(b) the product identification;
(c) the classification;
(d) the number of this European Standard;
(e) storage conditions;
(f) any relevant warnings.

title: 42 CFR Part 84 - Approval of Respiratory Protective Devices
title_zh: 42 CFR Part 84 - 呼吸防护设备的批准
issuing_authority: National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health (NIOSH)
summary: Federal regulation establishing requirements for the approval of respiratory protective devices by NIOSH, replacing the former Bureau of Mines schedule system.
summary_zh: 联邦法规，规定了NIOSH对呼吸防护设备的批准要求，取代了原矿业局计划系统。
full_text:
## Subpart A - General Provisions

### 84.1 - Purpose and scope
This part prescribes the requirements for the approval of respirators by the National Institute for Occupational Safety and Health (NIOSH), and establishes procedures to be followed in applying for such approval, and the minimum requirements that must be met in the manufacture and construction of respirators.

### 84.2 - Definitions
As used in this part:
(a) 'Approval' means a formal certification by NIOSH that a respirator has met the requirements of this part;
(b) 'Applicant' means any person who requests approval under this part;
(c) 'Respirator' means any device designed to provide respiratory protection to the wearer;
(d) 'Self-contained breathing apparatus (SCBA)' means a respirator that provides breathable air from a source carried by the user;
(e) 'Supplied-air respirator (SAR)' means a respirator that provides breathable air from a stationary source.

### 84.3 - Classification of respirators
Respirators shall be classified according to the following categories:

#### (a) Particulate respirators
(1) N-series: Not resistant to oil
(2) R-series: Resistant to oil
(3) P-series: Oil-proof

#### (b) Gas and vapor respirators
(1) Air-purifying respirators
(2) Atmosphere-supplying respirators

#### (c) Combination respirators
Respirators designed to protect against both particulates and gases/vapors.

### 84.4 - Particulate filter efficiency levels
Particulate respirators shall be classified according to their minimum filter efficiency as follows:

(a) 95% efficiency level (N95, R95, P95)
(b) 99% efficiency level (N99, R99, P99)
(c) 99.97% efficiency level (N100, R100, P100)

## Subpart B - Application for Approval

### 84.10 - Application procedures
(a) Any person may apply to NIOSH for approval of a respirator.
(b) The application shall be submitted in writing to the NIOSH National Personal Protective Technology Laboratory.
(c) The application shall include:
(1) A detailed description of the respirator;
(2) Drawings and specifications;
(3) Test data demonstrating compliance with the requirements of this part;
(4) A quality assurance plan;
(5) Labeling and instructions for use.

### 84.11 - Fees
(a) The applicant shall pay the fees prescribed in this section.
(b) The fees cover the costs of testing, evaluation, and approval activities.

## Subpart K - Non-Powered Air-Purifying Particulate Respirators

### 84.170 - Non-powered air-purifying particulate respirators; description
Non-powered air-purifying particulate respirators utilize the wearer's negative inhalation pressure to draw the ambient air through the air-purifying filter elements.

### 84.171 - Non-powered air-purifying particulate respirators; required components
(a) Each respirator shall consist of a facepiece and one or more filter elements.
(b) The facepiece shall be designed to form a seal with the wearer's face.
(c) Filter elements shall be replaceable or integral with the facepiece.

### 84.172 - Filter efficiency testing
(a) Test procedure. The filter efficiency shall be determined using a test aerosol of 0.3 micrometer diameter polystyrene latex particles or sodium chloride particles.

(b) Test conditions. Tests shall be conducted at a flow rate of 85 liters per minute.

(c) Performance requirements. The filter efficiency shall meet or exceed the minimum efficiency level specified in 84.4 for the applicable series and efficiency level.

### 84.173 - Exhalation valve leakage test
(a) Each respirator equipped with an exhalation valve shall be tested for valve leakage.
(b) The exhalation valve shall not leak more than 30 mL/min at a pressure of 1,000 Pa.

### 84.174 - Total inward leakage test
(a) The total inward leakage shall be measured on human test subjects performing exercises specified in the standard.
(b) The total inward leakage shall not exceed 10% for half-mask respirators and 2% for full-facepiece respirators.

### 84.175 - Breathing resistance test
(a) The inhalation resistance shall not exceed 35 mm water column at a flow rate of 85 L/min.
(b) The exhalation resistance shall not exceed 25 mm water column at a flow rate of 85 L/min.

## Subpart L - Powered Air-Purifying Particulate Respirators

### 84.180 - Powered air-purifying particulate respirators; description
Powered air-purifying particulate respirators (PAPRs) utilize a blower to force ambient air through air-purifying filter elements to the inlet covering.

### 84.181 - Required components
(a) Each PAPR shall consist of a blower, one or more filter elements, a breathing tube, and an inlet covering.
(b) The blower shall be powered by a battery or other portable power source.

### 84.182 - Filter efficiency testing
The filter efficiency requirements for PAPRs are the same as those specified in 84.172 for non-powered respirators.

## Subpart LL - Approval and Disapproval

### 84.990 - Issuance of approval
(a) NIOSH shall issue an approval if the respirator meets all applicable requirements.
(b) The approval shall include:
(1) The approval number;
(2) The respirator model and configuration;
(3) Any limitations or restrictions on use.

### 84.991 - Disapproval
(a) NIOSH shall disapprove a respirator if it fails to meet any applicable requirement.
(b) The applicant shall be notified in writing of the reasons for disapproval.

### 84.992 - Modification of approval
(a) The holder of an approval shall notify NIOSH of any modification to the approved respirator.
(b) NIOSH shall determine whether the modification requires a new approval.

## Subpart MM - Quality Assurance

### 84.1000 - Quality assurance requirements
(a) The manufacturer shall establish and maintain a quality assurance program.
(b) The quality assurance program shall include:
(1) Incoming inspection of raw materials and components;
(2) In-process inspection and testing;
(3) Final inspection and testing;
(4) Calibration of test equipment;
(5) Document control;
(6) Corrective action procedures.

### 84.1001 - Record keeping
(a) The manufacturer shall maintain records of all quality assurance activities.
(b) Records shall be maintained for a minimum of five years.
