title: GB 2626-2019 - Respiratory protective equipment - Non-powered air-purifying particle respirators
title_zh: GB 2626-2019 - 呼吸防护 自吸过滤式防颗粒物呼吸器
summary: Chinese national standard specifying technical requirements, testing methods, and marking for non-powered air-purifying particle respirators, replacing GB 2626-2006.
summary_zh: 中国国家标准，规定了自吸过滤式防颗粒物呼吸器的技术要求、测试方法和标识，替代GB 2626-2006。
full_text:
## 1 范围
本标准规定了自吸过滤式防颗粒物呼吸器的分类、技术要求、检测方法和标识。

本标准适用于防护各类颗粒物的自吸过滤式呼吸防护用品。

本标准不适用于防护有害气体和蒸气的呼吸防护用品，也不适用于缺氧环境、水下作业、逃生和消防用呼吸防护用品。

## 2 规范性引用文件
下列文件对于本文件的应用是必不可少的:
- GB 2890-2009 呼吸防护 自吸过滤式防毒面具
- GB/T 5703 用于技术设计的人体测量基础项目
- GB/T 10586 湿热试验箱技术条件
- GB/T 10589 低温试验箱技术条件
- GB/T 11158 高温试验箱技术条件
- GB/T 18664-2002 呼吸防护用品的选择、使用与维护

## 3 术语和定义
下列术语和定义适用于本文件。

### 3.1 自吸过滤式防颗粒物呼吸器
靠佩戴者呼吸克服部件气流阻力的过滤式呼吸器。

### 3.2 颗粒物
悬浮在空气中的固态、液态或固态与液态的颗粒状物质，如粉尘、烟、雾和微生物。

### 3.3 过滤效率
在规定检测条件下，过滤元件滤除颗粒物的百分比。

### 3.4 吸气阻力
在规定检测条件下，气体流经过滤元件及呼吸器其他部件时产生的压力降。

### 3.5 呼气阻力
在规定检测条件下，气体从佩戴者头部表面经呼吸器呼出时产生的压力降。

### 3.6 总泄漏率
在实验室规定检测条件下，受试者吸气时从包括过滤元件在内的所有面罩部件泄漏入面罩内的模拟剂浓度与吸入空气中模拟剂浓度的比值。

## 4 分类和标记

### 4.1 分类
自吸过滤式防颗粒物呼吸器按过滤性能分为KN和KP两类:

#### 4.1.1 KN类
适用于过滤非油性颗粒物。按过滤效率水平分为:
- KN90: 过滤效率 ≥ 90%
- KN95: 过滤效率 ≥ 95%
- KN100: 过滤效率 ≥ 99.97%

#### 4.1.2 KP类
适用于过滤油性和非油性颗粒物。按过滤效率水平分为:
- KP90: 过滤效率 ≥ 90%
- KP95: 过滤效率 ≥ 95%
- KP100: 过滤效率 ≥ 99.97%

### 4.2 标记
产品标记应包括以下内容:
(a) 产品名称和型号;
(b) 本标准编号 (GB 2626-2019);
(c) 过滤元件类别 (KN90、KN95、KN100、KP90、KP95、KP100);
(d) 制造商名称和地址;
(e) 生产日期或批号;
(f) 贮存期限;
(g) 使用说明。

## 5 技术要求

### 5.1 一般要求
#### 5.1.1 材料
呼吸器所使用的材料应满足以下要求:
(a) 直接与面部接触的材料不应对皮肤产生刺激性和过敏性;
(b) 过滤材料应质地均匀，不应有异常气味;
(c) 各部件所用的材料应具有足够的强度，在正常使用寿命中不应出现破损或变形。

#### 5.1.2 结构设计
(a) 呼吸器应不易产生结构性破损，部件的设计、组成和安装不应对使用者构成任何危险;
(b) 呼吸器的面罩设计应适合不同脸型的佩戴者，保证与面部的密合;
(c) 呼吸器应设有呼气阀，呼气阀应具有有效的防回吸功能。

### 5.2 过滤效率
过滤效率应符合表1的规定。

表1 过滤效率要求
| 类别 | 过滤效率 (%) |
|------|-------------|
| KN90 | ≥ 90.0 |
| KN95 | ≥ 95.0 |
| KN100 | ≥ 99.97 |
| KP90 | ≥ 90.0 |
| KP95 | ≥ 95.0 |
| KP100 | ≥ 99.97 |

### 5.3 泄漏性
#### 5.3.1 总泄漏率
以每个动作的 total inward leakage (TIL) 为评价基础时，各动作 TIL 的95%置信上限应不大于:
- KN90/KP90: 15%
- KN95/KP95: 11%
- KN100/KP100: 5%

#### 5.3.2 泄漏率
以人的总体 TIL 为评价基础时，各受试者总体 TIL 的95%置信上限应不大于:
- KN90/KP90: 15%
- KN95/KP95: 11%
- KN100/KP100: 5%

### 5.4 吸气阻力
吸气阻力不应大于表2规定的限值。

表2 吸气阻力要求
| 类别 | 吸气阻力 (Pa) |
|------|--------------|
| KN90/KP90 | ≤ 250 |
| KN95/KP95 | ≤ 300 |
| KN100/KP100 | ≤ 350 |

### 5.5 呼气阻力
呼气阻力不应大于 250 Pa。

### 5.6 呼气阀气密性
只检测半面罩。呼气阀片复原后，泄漏气流量不应大于 30 mL/min。

### 5.7 死腔
吸入气中二氧化碳体积分数不应大于 1%。

### 5.8 视野
下方视野不应小于 60°。

### 5.9 头带
头带应具有一定的弹性，佩戴方便，并应能将面罩牢固地固定在脸上。

### 5.10 连接和连接部件
连接和连接部件应具有一定的强度，在正常使用中不应出现滑脱、断裂或变形。

### 5.11 镜片
镜片应满足以下要求:
(a) 镜片不应出现影响视线的气泡、划痕、杂质等缺陷;
(b) 镜片应能承受一定的冲击。

### 5.12 气密性
全面罩的气密性应满足: 在测试条件下，泄漏气流量不应大于 100 mL/min。

### 5.13 可燃性
暴露于火焰的各部件在从火焰移开后，不应燃烧; 如果燃烧，续燃时间不应超过 5s。

### 5.14 清洗和消毒
制造商应提供适用的清洗和消毒方法，经5次清洗和消毒后，过滤效率应仍符合5.2的要求，吸气阻力应仍符合5.4的要求。

### 5.15 实用性能
经佩戴者评价，呼吸器应易于佩戴和摘脱，在佩戴过程中无不适感和压迫感，在低头、转头、说话和弯腰等动作时，呼吸器不应发生移位或脱落。

## 6 检测方法

### 6.1 检测条件
除非特别说明，检测应在以下环境条件下进行:
- 温度: (25 ± 5)°C
- 相对湿度: (30 ± 10)%

### 6.2 过滤效率检测
使用氯化钠颗粒物或油性颗粒物作为测试介质，颗粒物浓度和粒径分布应符合标准要求。

### 6.3 泄漏性检测
使用人体试验对象进行测试，测试动作包括正常呼吸、深呼吸、左右转头、上下活动头部、说话、弯腰、左右摇头和正常行走。

### 6.4 吸气阻力和呼气阻力检测
使用呼吸模拟器或人体试验对象进行测试，测试流量应符合标准要求。

## 7 标识

### 7.1 产品标识
每个呼吸器上应清晰、永久地标明:
(a) 制造商名称或商标;
(b) 产品型号;
(c) 执行标准编号 (GB 2626-2019);
(d) 过滤元件类别;
(e) 生产日期或批号。

### 7.2 包装标识
包装应标明:
(a) 产品名称和型号;
(b) 执行标准编号;
(c) 过滤元件类别;
(d) 制造商名称和地址;
(e) 生产日期;
(f) 贮存期限;
(g) 使用说明和注意事项。

## 8 包装、运输和贮存

### 8.1 包装
产品应密封包装，包装应能防止机械损坏和使用前的污染。

### 8.2 运输
运输过程中应避免重压、阳光直射和雨雪浸淋。

### 8.3 贮存
产品应贮存在阴凉干燥处，避免与酸、碱及其他有毒有害物品混贮。

title: EN ISO 20345:2022 - Personal protective equipment - Safety footwear
title_zh: EN ISO 20345:2022 - 个人防护装备 - 安全鞋
summary: European standard specifying basic and additional requirements for safety footwear used for general professional use, including toe protection against impact and compression.
summary_zh: 欧洲标准，规定了通用专业用途安全鞋的基本和附加要求，包括防冲击和压缩的足趾保护。
full_text:
## 1 Scope
This document specifies basic and additional (optional) requirements for safety footwear used for general professional use.

This document does not cover:
(a) footwear for special risk applications such as firefighting or chainsaw use;
(b) footwear for extreme environments such as deep-sea diving or high-voltage electrical work.

## 2 Normative references
- EN ISO 20344:2021, Personal protective equipment - Test methods for footwear
- EN ISO 20346:2021, Personal protective equipment - Protective footwear
- EN ISO 20347:2021, Personal protective equipment - Occupational footwear

## 3 Terms and definitions
### 3.1 safety footwear
Footwear incorporating protective features to protect the wearer from injuries which could arise through accidents, fitted with toe caps designed to give protection against impact when tested at an energy level of at least 200 J and against compression when tested at a compression load of at least 15 kN.

### 3.2 protective toecap
A built-in feature designed to protect the wearer's toes from falling objects and compression.

### 3.3 penetration-resistant insert
A built-in feature designed to prevent sharp objects from penetrating the sole.

## 4 Classification
Safety footwear shall be classified according to the upper material:

### 4.1 Class I
Footwear made from leather and other materials, excluding all-rubber or all-polymeric footwear.

### 4.2 Class II
All-rubber (i.e., entirely vulcanised) or all-polymeric (i.e., entirely moulded) footwear.

## 5 Basic requirements

### 5.1 Design and construction
Safety footwear shall be designed and constructed to provide protection against the risks for which it is intended.

### 5.2 Toe protection
#### 5.2.1 Impact resistance
The toecap shall provide protection against impact of at least 200 J.

#### 5.2.2 Compression resistance
The toecap shall provide protection against compression of at least 15 kN.

### 5.3 Upper materials
The upper materials shall meet the requirements for:
(a) Tear strength;
(b) Abrasion resistance;
(c) Flexing resistance.

### 5.4 Outsole requirements
The outsole shall meet the requirements for:
(a) Abrasion resistance;
(b) Flexing resistance;
(c) Oil and fuel resistance (where claimed).

## 6 Additional requirements

### 6.1 Closed heel area
The footwear shall have a closed heel area.

### 6.2 Energy absorption of seat region
The seat region shall have an energy absorption of at least 20 J.

### 6.3 Antistatic properties
Where claimed, the footwear shall have antistatic properties with a resistance between 100 kΩ and 1000 MΩ.

### 6.4 Penetration resistance
Where fitted with a penetration-resistant insert, the insert shall withstand a nail penetration force of at least 1100 N.

### 6.5 Electrical properties
#### 6.5.1 Conductive footwear
Resistance shall not exceed 100 kΩ.

#### 6.5.2 Antistatic footwear
Resistance shall be between 100 kΩ and 1000 MΩ.

#### 6.5.3 Electrically insulating footwear
Shall withstand a test voltage of at least 18 kV.

### 6.6 Thermal insulation of sole complex
The sole complex shall provide thermal insulation when tested on a hot surface of 150°C or 250°C.

### 6.7 Water resistance
Where claimed, the footwear shall provide water resistance when tested in accordance with EN ISO 20344.

### 6.8 Metatarsal protection
Where fitted, metatarsal protection shall withstand an impact of at least 100 J.

### 6.9 Ankle protection
Where fitted, ankle protection shall reduce the impact force by at least 50%.

### 6.10 Cut resistance
Where claimed, the footwear shall provide cut resistance when tested in accordance with EN ISO 20344.

## 7 Marking

### 7.1 Required marking
Each safety footwear shall be marked with:
(a) The manufacturer's name or identifying mark;
(b) The year of manufacture;
(c) The standard number (EN ISO 20345:2022);
(d) The classification (SB, S1, S1P, S2, S3, S4, S5);
(e) The manufacturer's type or model identification.

### 7.2 Classification marking
The following classification codes shall be used:
- SB: Basic impact and compression protection
- S1: SB + closed heel + antistatic + energy absorption
- S1P: S1 + penetration resistance
- S2: S1 + water penetration and absorption resistance
- S3: S2 + penetration resistance + cleated outsole
- S4: All polymer/rubber footwear with S1 properties
- S5: All polymer/rubber footwear with S3 properties

## 8 Information supplied by the manufacturer

### 8.1 Instructions for use
The manufacturer shall provide instructions containing:
(a) Identification of the product;
(b) Intended use and limitations;
(c) Fitting instructions;
(d) Cleaning and maintenance instructions;
(e) Storage conditions;
(f) Warnings and precautions.

### 8.2 Packaging
The packaging shall be marked with:
(a) The manufacturer's name and address;
(b) The product identification;
(c) The classification;
(d) The standard number;
(e) Size marking.

title: ASTM F2413-18 - Standard Specification for Performance Requirements for Protective (Safety) Toe Cap Footwear
title_zh: ASTM F2413-18 - 防护(安全)鞋头鞋的性能要求标准规范
summary: ASTM standard specifying the minimum performance requirements for protective footwear including toe protection, metatarsal protection, and conductive properties.
summary_zh: ASTM标准，规定了防护鞋的最低性能要求，包括足趾保护、跖骨保护和导电性能。
full_text:
## 1. Scope

1.1 This specification covers minimum requirements for the performance of footwear to provide protection against a variety of workplace hazards that can potentially result in injury.

1.2 This specification is not intended to serve as a detailed manufacturing or purchasing specification, but can be referenced in purchase contracts to ensure that minimum performance requirements are met.

1.3 Controlled laboratory tests used to determine compliance with the performance requirements of this specification shall not be deemed as establishing performance levels for all situations to which individuals may be exposed.

## 2. Referenced Documents

2.1 ASTM Standards:
- F1116 Test Method for Electrical Protective Footwear for Temporary Grounding
- F1117 Specification for Dielectric Footwear
- F1818 Specification for Foot Protection for Chain Saw Users
- F2892 Specification for Performance Requirements for Soft Toe Protective Footwear

2.2 ANSI Standards:
- Z41-1999 American National Standard for Personal Protection - Protective Footwear

## 3. Terminology

3.1 Definitions of Terms Specific to This Standard:

3.1.1 protective (safety) toe cap footwear - Footwear designed to protect the wearer's toes from impact and compression hazards.

3.1.2 impact resistance - The ability of the toe cap to withstand a falling object.

3.1.3 compression resistance - The ability of the toe cap to withstand a rolling object.

3.1.4 metatarsal guard - A protective device designed to protect the metatarsal bones of the foot.

3.1.5 conductive properties - The ability of the footwear to conduct static electricity.

3.1.6 static dissipative properties - The ability of the footwear to reduce the accumulation of excess static electricity.

3.1.7 electrical hazard protection - The ability of the footwear to protect the wearer from electrical shocks.

## 4. Classification

4.1 Impact Resistance for the Toe Area of Footwear:

4.1.1 I/75 - Footwear built with a toe cap capable of withstanding an impact of 75 foot-pounds (101.7 J).

4.1.2 I/50 - Footwear built with a toe cap capable of withstanding an impact of 50 foot-pounds (67.8 J).

4.1.3 I/30 - Footwear built with a toe cap capable of withstanding an impact of 30 foot-pounds (40.7 J).

4.2 Compression Resistance for the Toe Area of Footwear:

4.2.1 C/75 - Footwear capable of withstanding a compression force of 2500 pounds (11,121 N).

4.2.2 C/50 - Footwear capable of withstanding a compression force of 1750 pounds (7,785 N).

4.2.3 C/30 - Footwear capable of withstanding a compression force of 1000 pounds (4,448 N).

4.3 Metatarsal Impact Resistance:

4.3.1 Mt/75 - Footwear with a metatarsal guard capable of withstanding an impact of 75 foot-pounds (101.7 J).

4.3.2 Mt/50 - Footwear with a metatarsal guard capable of withstanding an impact of 50 foot-pounds (67.8 J).

4.4 Conductive Properties:

4.4.1 Cd - Footwear with conductive properties capable of discharging static electricity.

4.5 Static Dissipative Properties:

4.5.1 SD - Footwear with static dissipative properties.

4.6 Electrical Hazard Protection:

4.6.1 EH - Footwear designed to provide protection against electrical hazards.

4.7 Puncture Resistance:

4.7.1 PR - Footwear with puncture-resistant soles.

## 5. Performance Requirements

5.1 Impact Resistance Test:

5.1.1 Test Method - The impact resistance shall be determined by dropping a 50-pound (22.7 kg) weight from a predetermined height onto the toe cap.

5.1.2 Performance Requirements:
(a) I/75 - The clearance after impact shall not be less than 16.0 mm (5/8 inch).
(b) I/50 - The clearance after impact shall not be less than 16.0 mm (5/8 inch).
(c) I/30 - The clearance after impact shall not be less than 16.0 mm (5/8 inch).

5.2 Compression Resistance Test:

5.2.1 Test Method - The compression resistance shall be determined by applying a compressive load to the toe cap at a rate of 1000 pounds (4448 N) per minute.

5.2.2 Performance Requirements:
(a) C/75 - The clearance after compression shall not be less than 16.0 mm (5/8 inch).
(b) C/50 - The clearance after compression shall not be less than 16.0 mm (5/8 inch).
(c) C/30 - The clearance after compression shall not be less than 16.0 mm (5/8 inch).

5.3 Metatarsal Impact Resistance Test:

5.3.1 Test Method - The metatarsal impact resistance shall be determined by dropping a 50-pound (22.7 kg) weight from a predetermined height onto the metatarsal guard.

5.3.2 Performance Requirements:
(a) Mt/75 - The clearance after impact shall not be less than 25.4 mm (1 inch).
(b) Mt/50 - The clearance after impact shall not be less than 25.4 mm (1 inch).

5.4 Conductive Properties Test:

5.4.1 Test Method - The electrical resistance shall be measured between electrodes placed on the insole and outsole of the footwear.

5.4.2 Performance Requirements:
The electrical resistance shall not exceed 500,000 ohms (500 kΩ).

5.5 Static Dissipative Properties Test:

5.5.1 Test Method - The electrical resistance shall be measured between electrodes placed on the insole and outsole of the footwear.

5.5.2 Performance Requirements:
The electrical resistance shall be between 1,000,000 ohms (1 MΩ) and 100,000,000 ohms (100 MΩ).

5.6 Electrical Hazard Protection Test:

5.6.1 Test Method - The footwear shall be tested for dielectric strength by applying a test voltage of 18,000 volts at 60 Hz for 1 minute.

5.6.2 Performance Requirements:
The leakage current shall not exceed 1.0 mA.

5.7 Puncture Resistance Test:

5.7.1 Test Method - The puncture resistance shall be determined by pressing a sharp nail through the sole at a rate of 0.5 inches (12.7 mm) per minute.

5.7.2 Performance Requirements:
The puncture-resistant device shall withstand a force of at least 270 pounds (1200 N).

## 6. Marking and Labeling

6.1 Each article of protective footwear shall be marked with the following information:

(a) The ASTM designation (F2413-18);
(b) The specific year of the standard;
(c) The impact resistance rating (I/75, I/50, or I/30);
(d) The compression resistance rating (C/75, C/50, or C/30);
(e) Any additional protective properties (Mt, Cd, SD, EH, PR).

6.2 The marking shall be clearly visible and permanent.

## 7. Certification

7.1 The manufacturer shall certify that the protective footwear meets the requirements of this specification.

7.2 The certification shall include:
(a) The manufacturer's name and address;
(b) The model number and description;
(c) The test results;
(d) The date of manufacture.

## 8. Quality Assurance

8.1 The manufacturer shall maintain a quality assurance program to ensure that all protective footwear meets the requirements of this specification.

8.2 The quality assurance program shall include:
(a) Incoming inspection of materials;
(b) In-process inspection;
(c) Final inspection and testing;
(d) Calibration of test equipment;
(e) Record keeping.

title: EN ISO 374-1:2016+A1:2018 - Protective gloves against dangerous chemicals and microorganisms
title_zh: EN ISO 374-1:2016+A1:2018 - 防危险化学品和微生物的防护手套
summary: European standard specifying the requirements for protective gloves intended to protect the user against dangerous chemicals and/or microorganisms.
summary_zh: 欧洲标准，规定了旨在保护使用者免受危险化学品和/或微生物侵害的防护手套的要求。
full_text:
## 1 Scope
This document specifies the requirements for protective gloves intended to protect the user against dangerous chemicals and/or microorganisms.

This document does not specify requirements for protection against mechanical risks, which are covered by EN 388.

## 2 Normative references
- EN 388:2016+A1:2018, Protective gloves against mechanical risks
- EN 420:2003+A1:2009, Protective gloves - General requirements and test methods
- EN ISO 374-2:2019, Protective gloves against dangerous chemicals and microorganisms - Part 2: Determination of resistance to penetration
- EN ISO 374-4:2019, Protective gloves against dangerous chemicals and microorganisms - Part 4: Determination of resistance to degradation by chemicals
- EN ISO 374-5:2016, Protective gloves against dangerous chemicals and microorganisms - Part 5: Terminology and performance requirements for microorganisms risk

## 3 Terms and definitions
### 3.1 protective glove
A glove designed to protect the hand or part of the hand against one or more specific risks.

### 3.2 chemical protective glove
A protective glove designed to protect the hand against one or more specific chemicals.

### 3.3 breakthrough time
The elapsed time between initial contact of the chemical with the outside surface of the glove and the time at which the chemical can be detected at the inside surface of the glove.

### 3.4 degradation rating
A qualitative assessment of the change in one or more physical properties of the glove material due to contact with a chemical.

### 3.5 microorganism
A microbiological entity, including bacteria, viruses, fungi, and parasites.

## 4 Classification

### 4.1 Type A
Gloves providing protection against permeation by chemicals with a breakthrough time of at least 30 minutes for at least 6 test chemicals.

### 4.2 Type B
Gloves providing protection against permeation by chemicals with a breakthrough time of at least 30 minutes for at least 3 test chemicals.

### 4.3 Type C
Gloves providing protection against permeation by chemicals with a breakthrough time of at least 10 minutes for at least 1 test chemical.

## 5 Requirements

### 5.1 General requirements
Chemical protective gloves shall comply with the general requirements specified in EN 420.

### 5.2 Chemical permeation resistance
#### 5.2.1 Test chemicals
The following test chemicals shall be used:
(a) Methanol (Code A);
(b) Acetone (Code B);
(c) Acetonitrile (Code C);
(d) Dichloromethane (Code D);
(e) Carbon disulfide (Code E);
(f) Toluene (Code F);
(g) Diethylamine (Code G);
(h) Tetrahydrofuran (Code H);
(i) Ethyl acetate (Code I);
(j) n-Heptane (Code J);
(k) Sodium hydroxide 40% (Code K);
(l) Sulphuric acid 96% (Code L).

#### 5.2.2 Performance levels
The performance level shall be determined by the breakthrough time:

| Performance Level | Breakthrough Time (minutes) |
|-------------------|----------------------------|
| Level 1 | > 10 |
| Level 2 | > 30 |
| Level 3 | > 60 |
| Level 4 | > 120 |
| Level 5 | > 240 |
| Level 6 | > 480 |

### 5.3 Chemical degradation resistance
The degradation resistance shall be determined in accordance with EN ISO 374-4.

### 5.4 Microorganism resistance
#### 5.4.1 Bacteria and fungi
Gloves claiming protection against bacteria and fungi shall meet the requirements of EN ISO 374-2.

#### 5.4.2 Viruses
Gloves claiming protection against viruses shall meet the requirements of EN ISO 374-2 and shall pass the additional penetration test specified in Annex A.

## 6 Marking

### 6.1 Pictogram
Chemical protective gloves shall bear the pictogram indicating protection against chemicals.

### 6.2 Code letters
The code letters of the test chemicals against which the glove provides protection shall be indicated below the pictogram.

### 6.3 Type indication
The type (A, B, or C) shall be indicated on the glove and packaging.

## 7 Information supplied by the manufacturer

### 7.1 User instructions
The manufacturer shall provide instructions containing:
(a) The type and level of protection provided;
(b) The chemicals against which the glove provides protection;
(c) Instructions for donning and doffing;
(d) Limitations of use;
(e) Storage conditions;
(f) Shelf life.

### 7.2 Degradation and permeation data
The manufacturer shall provide degradation and permeation data for the chemicals listed in 5.2.1.

title: FDA Guidance for Industry and FDA Staff - Medical Gloves
title_zh: FDA行业指南和FDA工作人员 - 医用手套
summary: FDA guidance document for medical gloves (patient examination gloves and surgeons' gloves), including premarket notification requirements and testing standards.
summary_zh: FDA医用手套（患者检查手套和外科医生手套）指南文件，包括上市前通知要求和测试标准。
full_text:
## I. Introduction

This guidance document provides recommendations for the content and format of premarket notification (510(k)) submissions for medical gloves. This guidance applies to:

(a) Patient examination gloves (21 CFR 880.6250);
(b) Surgeon's gloves (21 CFR 878.4460).

## II. Device Description

### A. Patient Examination Gloves
Patient examination gloves are disposable devices intended for medical purposes that are worn on the examiner's hands to prevent contamination between patient and examiner.

### B. Surgeon's Gloves
Surgeon's gloves are disposable devices intended for surgical purposes that are worn on the hands of operating room personnel to prevent contamination between the surgical staff and the patient.

## III. Classification and Regulation

### A. Patient Examination Gloves
- Classification: Class I (reserved)
- Product Code: LYZ
- Regulation: 21 CFR 880.6250

### B. Surgeon's Gloves
- Classification: Class I (reserved)
- Product Code: LZA
- Regulation: 21 CFR 878.4460

## IV. Predicate Device Comparison

The 510(k) submission shall include a comparison between the subject device and a legally marketed predicate device. The comparison shall address:

(a) Intended use;
(b) Material composition;
(c) Dimensions and sizing;
(d) Sterility claims;
(e) Powder claims;
(f) Color and texture;
(g) Performance specifications.

## V. Performance Testing

### A. Biocompatibility Testing
Medical gloves shall be tested for biocompatibility in accordance with ISO 10993.

Required tests include:
(a) Cytotoxicity (ISO 10993-5);
(b) Sensitization (ISO 10993-10);
(c) Irritation (ISO 10993-10).

### B. Physical Property Testing
#### 1. Dimensions
Glove dimensions shall comply with ASTM D3578 (natural rubber) or ASTM D6319 (nitrile) or ASTM D5250 (polyvinyl chloride).

#### 2. Tensile Strength and Ultimate Elongation
The tensile strength and ultimate elongation shall be tested in accordance with ASTM D412.

Minimum requirements:
- Natural rubber latex gloves: Tensile strength ≥ 18 MPa; Ultimate elongation ≥ 650%
- Nitrile gloves: Tensile strength ≥ 14 MPa; Ultimate elongation ≥ 500%
- PVC gloves: Tensile strength ≥ 11 MPa; Ultimate elongation ≥ 300%

#### 3. Force at Break
The force at break shall be tested in accordance with ASTM D412.

#### 4. Thickness
The thickness shall be measured in accordance with ASTM D3767.

### C. Barrier Integrity Testing
#### 1. Water Leak Test
The water leak test shall be conducted in accordance with ASTM D5151.

Acceptance criteria: The acceptable quality level (AQL) shall not exceed 2.5.

#### 2. Viral Penetration Test (for surgeon's gloves)
The viral penetration test shall be conducted in accordance with ASTM F1671.

### D. Powder Content Testing
For powdered gloves, the powder content shall be determined in accordance with ASTM D6124.

For powder-free gloves, the powder content shall not exceed 2.0 mg per glove.

### E. Protein Content Testing (for natural rubber latex gloves)
The extractable protein content shall be determined in accordance with ASTM D5712.

The protein content shall be reported but no specific limit is required.

### F. Chemical Residue Testing
Residual chemicals shall be tested and reported, including:
(a) Accelerators;
(b) Antioxidants;
(c) Processing aids.

## VI. Sterility Validation

For sterile gloves, the sterilization process shall be validated in accordance with ISO 11137 or ANSI/AAMI ST67.

The sterility assurance level (SAL) shall be 10^-6.

## VII. Shelf Life Validation

The shelf life shall be validated through accelerated aging studies in accordance with ASTM F1980.

The claimed shelf life shall be supported by real-time aging data.

## VIII. Labeling

### A. Package Label
The package label shall include:
(a) The manufacturer's name and address;
(b) The product name and description;
(c) The size;
(d) The material (e.g., natural rubber latex, nitrile, vinyl);
(e) Sterility status (if applicable);
(f) Powder status (powdered or powder-free);
(g) Single-use statement;
(h) Storage conditions;
(i) Expiration date;
(j) Lot number.

### B. Warnings and Precautions
The labeling shall include the following warnings:
(a) For natural rubber latex gloves: 'Caution: This product contains natural rubber latex which may cause allergic reactions.'
(b) For powder-free gloves: 'Powder-free' claim.
(c) For sterile gloves: Sterility assurance statement.

### C. Instructions for Use
The instructions for use shall include:
(a) Intended use;
(b) Contraindications;
(c) Warnings and precautions;
(d) Instructions for donning and doffing;
(e) Storage conditions.

## IX. Quality System Regulation

Manufacturers shall comply with the Quality System Regulation (21 CFR Part 820).

## X. Special Considerations

### A. Powder-Free Gloves
The FDA recommends the use of powder-free gloves to minimize the risk of allergic reactions and complications associated with glove powder.

### B. Latex Allergies
Manufacturers shall provide clear labeling regarding latex content and potential allergic reactions.

### C. Double-Gloving
For surgical procedures, the FDA recognizes the practice of double-gloving to reduce the risk of glove failure.

title: EN 166:2001 - Personal eye-protection - Specifications
title_zh: EN 166:2001 - 个人眼睛防护 - 规范
summary: European standard specifying the functional requirements and test methods for eye protectors used in various occupational applications.
summary_zh: 欧洲标准，规定了用于各种职业应用的护眼器的功能要求和测试方法。
full_text:
## 1 Scope
This standard specifies the functional requirements and test methods for eye protectors used in various occupational applications.

This standard does not apply to:
(a) Eye protectors for use with corrective spectacle lenses;
(b) Eye protectors for protection against radiation from nuclear sources;
(c) Eye protectors for medical purposes.

## 2 Normative references
- EN 167:2001, Personal eye-protection - Optical test methods
- EN 168:2001, Personal eye-protection - Non-optical test methods
- EN 169:2002, Personal eye-protection - Filters for welding and related techniques
- EN 170:2002, Personal eye-protection - Ultraviolet filters
- EN 171:2002, Personal eye-protection - Infrared filters
- EN 172:1994, Personal eye-protection - Sun-glare filters for industrial use
- EN 175:1997, Personal protection - Equipment for eye and face protection during welding and allied processes

## 3 Definitions
### 3.1 eye protector
A device designed to provide protection to the eyes and/or face against one or more specific hazards.

### 3.2 ocular
The part of the eye protector through which the wearer sees.

### 3.3 frame
The part of the eye protector that holds the ocular(s) in position.

### 3.4 side-shield
A component designed to provide lateral protection to the eye.

### 3.5 filter
An optical device that reduces the intensity of radiation in one or more parts of the electromagnetic spectrum.

## 4 Classification of eye protectors

### 4.1 By type of protection
(a) Spectacles (type 1);
(b) Goggles (type 2);
(c) Face-shields (type 3).

### 4.2 By field of application
(a) Basic impact protection (F);
(b) Increased robustness (B);
(c) High-speed particles, low energy (A);
(d) High-speed particles, medium energy (B);
(e) High-speed particles, high energy (C);
(f) High-speed particles at extreme temperatures (T).

### 4.3 By optical class
(a) Optical class 1: For continuous work;
(b) Optical class 2: For intermittent work;
(c) Optical class 3: For occasional work.

## 5 Requirements

### 5.1 Field of vision
The field of vision shall not be unduly restricted by the eye protector.

### 5.2 Optical requirements
#### 5.2.1 Refractive power
The refractive power shall not exceed ±0.06 dioptres for optical class 1, ±0.12 dioptres for optical class 2, and ±0.18 dioptres for optical class 3.

#### 5.2.2 Astigmatism
The astigmatism shall not exceed 0.06 dioptres for optical class 1, 0.12 dioptres for optical class 2, and 0.18 dioptres for optical class 3.

#### 5.2.3 Prismatic power
The prismatic power shall not exceed 0.25 cm/m for optical class 1, 0.37 cm/m for optical class 2, and 0.50 cm/m for optical class 3.

#### 5.2.4 Diffusion
The diffusion shall not exceed the values specified in EN 167.

#### 5.2.5 Transmittance
The luminous transmittance shall be within the range specified for the particular type of filter.

### 5.3 Mechanical strength
#### 5.3.1 Minimum robustness
The ocular shall withstand the minimum robustness test (5 m/s impact).

#### 5.3.2 Increased robustness
The ocular shall withstand the increased robustness test (12 m/s impact).

#### 5.3.3 High-speed particles
The ocular shall withstand the high-speed particle test at the appropriate energy level (45 m/s, 120 m/s, or 190 m/s).

### 5.4 Resistance to radiation
The ocular shall not undergo significant changes in transmittance after exposure to ultraviolet radiation.

### 5.5 Resistance to high temperatures
The eye protector shall not deform or ignite when exposed to a radiant heat source of 650°C.

### 5.6 Protection against droplets and splashes
The eye protector shall prevent the penetration of liquids.

### 5.7 Protection against dust
The eye protector shall prevent the ingress of dust particles.

### 5.8 Protection against gases and fine dust
The eye protector shall provide a seal against gases and fine dust particles.

### 5.9 Electrical properties
The eye protector shall not conduct electricity when tested in accordance with EN 168.

### 5.10 Surface quality
The ocular shall be free from defects that could impair vision.

## 6 Marking

### 6.1 Required marking
Each eye protector shall be marked with:
(a) The manufacturer's name or trademark;
(b) The number of this standard (EN 166);
(c) The symbol for the field of application;
(d) The optical class;
(e) The symbol for the mechanical strength.

### 6.2 Additional marking
Where applicable, the eye protector shall also be marked with:
(a) The symbol for high-temperature resistance (T);
(b) The symbol for chemical resistance (C);
(c) The symbol for electrical protection (E).

## 7 Information supplied by the manufacturer

### 7.1 User instructions
The manufacturer shall provide instructions containing:
(a) The intended use and limitations;
(b) Instructions for fitting and adjustment;
(c) Cleaning and disinfection instructions;
(d) Storage conditions;
(e) Warnings and precautions.

### 7.2 Packaging
The packaging shall be marked with:
(a) The manufacturer's name and address;
(b) The product identification;
(c) The standard number;
(d) The classification markings.

title: ANSI/ISEA Z87.1-2020 - American National Standard for Occupational and Educational Personal Eye and Face Protection Devices
title_zh: ANSI/ISEA Z87.1-2020 - 职业和教育个人眼面部防护设备美国国家标准
summary: American national standard establishing criteria for testing and selecting eye and face protection devices to prevent or minimize injuries from occupational and educational hazards.
summary_zh: 美国国家标准，规定了测试和选择眼面部防护设备的标准，以防止或最大限度地减少职业和教育危害造成的伤害。
full_text:
## 1. Scope and purpose

### 1.1 Scope
This standard sets forth criteria related to the general requirements, testing, permanent marking, selection, care, and use of protectors to minimize the occurrence of or lessen the severity of injuries to eyes and faces from selected hazards.

### 1.2 Purpose
The purpose of this standard is to provide minimum requirements for eye and face protectors selected by employers and used by employees in the workplace.

## 2. Definitions

### 2.1 eye protector
A device designed to provide protection to the eyes against one or more specific hazards.

### 2.2 face protector
A device designed to provide protection to the face and eyes against one or more specific hazards.

### 2.3 plano protector
An eye or face protector that does not incorporate a corrective lens.

### 2.4 prescription protector
An eye or face protector that incorporates a corrective lens.

### 2.5 impact resistance
The ability of a protector to withstand impact from flying objects.

### 2.6 lens
The transparent or translucent part of an eye protector through which the wearer sees.

## 3. Requirements

### 3.1 Impact protection
#### 3.1.1 Basic impact
Protectors shall be capable of resisting impact from a 25.4 mm (1 inch) diameter steel ball dropped from a height of 127 cm (50 inches).

#### 3.1.2 High mass impact
Protectors shall be capable of resisting impact from a 500 g (17.6 oz) pointed projectile dropped from a height of 127 cm (50 inches).

#### 3.1.3 High velocity impact
Protectors shall be capable of resisting impact from a 6.35 mm (0.25 inch) steel ball traveling at specified velocities:
(a) Spectacles: 45.7 m/s (150 ft/s)
(b) Goggles: 76.2 m/s (250 ft/s)
(c) Face shields: 91.4 m/s (300 ft/s)

### 3.2 Optical requirements
#### 3.2.1 Refractive power
The refractive power shall not exceed ±0.09 dioptres for plano protectors.

#### 3.2.2 Astigmatism
The astigmatism shall not exceed 0.09 dioptres for plano protectors.

#### 3.2.3 Prismatic power
The horizontal prismatic power shall not exceed 0.25 cm/m.

#### 3.2.4 Haze
The haze shall not exceed 1% for clear lenses.

#### 3.2.5 Luminous transmittance
The luminous transmittance shall be within the range specified for the particular type of lens.

### 3.3 Coverage
Protectors shall provide adequate coverage to protect the eyes and surrounding areas.

### 3.4 Lens thickness
The minimum lens thickness shall be:
(a) Spectacles: 2.0 mm
(b) Goggles: 2.0 mm
(c) Face shields: 1.0 mm
(d) Welding filters: 1.8 mm

### 3.5 Corrosion resistance
Metal components shall be corrosion-resistant.

### 3.6 Ignition resistance
Protectors shall not ignite when exposed to a flame for 5 seconds.

### 3.7 Lens retention
Lenses shall be securely retained in the frame under impact conditions.

## 4. Testing

### 4.1 Impact tests
#### 4.1.1 Drop ball test
A 25.4 mm steel ball shall be dropped from a height of 127 cm onto the lens. The lens shall not fracture, and the lens shall not be displaced from the frame.

#### 4.1.2 High mass impact test
A 500 g pointed projectile shall be dropped from a height of 127 cm. The protector shall not fracture, and no part shall contact the eye or head form.

#### 4.1.3 High velocity impact test
A 6.35 mm steel ball shall be propelled at the specified velocity. The lens shall not fracture, and no part shall contact the eye form.

### 4.2 Optical tests
Optical tests shall be conducted in accordance with the procedures specified in Annex A.

### 4.3 Coverage test
The coverage shall be verified using the head forms specified in Annex B.

## 5. Marking

### 5.1 Required marking
Each protector shall be marked with:
(a) The manufacturer's name or trademark;
(b) The 'Z87' or 'Z87+' marking indicating compliance with this standard;
(c) The lens type (if other than clear).

### 5.2 Impact rating marking
Protectors meeting the high velocity impact requirements shall be marked with 'Z87+'.

Protectors meeting only the basic impact requirements shall be marked with 'Z87'.

### 5.3 Special purpose lens marking
Special purpose lenses shall be marked with:
(a) 'U' followed by the scale number for ultraviolet filters;
(b) 'R' followed by the scale number for infrared filters;
(c) 'V' for variable tint lenses;
(d) 'S' for special purpose lenses.

## 6. Selection guide

### 6.1 General considerations
The selection of appropriate eye and face protection shall be based on:
(a) The type of hazard;
(b) The severity of the hazard;
(c) The conditions of exposure;
(d) The visual requirements of the task;
(e) The personal characteristics of the wearer.

### 6.2 Hazard assessment
Employers shall conduct a hazard assessment to identify potential eye and face hazards in the workplace.

### 6.3 Selection by hazard type
#### 6.3.1 Impact hazards
For impact hazards, select protectors marked 'Z87+'.

#### 6.3.2 Heat hazards
For heat hazards, select protectors with heat-resistant properties.

#### 6.3.3 Chemical hazards
For chemical hazards, select goggles or face shields with appropriate chemical resistance.

#### 6.3.4 Dust hazards
For dust hazards, select tight-fitting goggles or face shields.

#### 6.3.5 Optical radiation hazards
For optical radiation hazards, select protectors with appropriate filter lenses.

## 7. Care and maintenance

### 7.1 Cleaning
Protectors shall be cleaned regularly using mild soap and water or lens cleaning solutions.

### 7.2 Inspection
Protectors shall be inspected before each use for damage or defects.

### 7.3 Storage
Protectors shall be stored in a clean, dry place away from direct sunlight.

### 7.4 Replacement
Protectors shall be replaced when damaged, scratched, or when the lens becomes pitted.

title: EN 397:2012+A1:2012 - Industrial safety helmets
summary: European standard specifying physical and performance requirements, methods of test and marking requirements for industrial safety helmets.
summary_zh: 欧洲标准，规定了工业安全帽的物理和性能要求、测试方法和标识要求。
full_text:
## 1 Scope
This European Standard specifies physical and performance requirements, methods of test and marking requirements for industrial safety helmets.

The standard is applicable to safety helmets designed to protect the upper part of the wearer's head against the effects of falling objects and the resulting impact, and to protect against certain hazards.

## 2 Normative references
- EN 960:2006, Headforms for use in the testing of protective helmets
- EN 13087-1:2000, Protective helmets - Test methods - Part 1: Conditions and conditioning
- EN 13087-2:2000, Protective helmets - Test methods - Part 2: Shock absorption
- EN 13087-3:2000, Protective helmets - Test methods - Part 3: Resistance to penetration
- EN 13087-4:2001, Protective helmets - Test methods - Part 4: Retention system effectiveness
- EN 13087-5:2000, Protective helmets - Test methods - Part 5: Retention system strength
- EN 13087-6:2001, Protective helmets - Test methods - Part 6: Field of vision
- EN 13087-7:2001, Protective helmets - Test methods - Part 7: Flame resistance
- EN 13087-8:2001, Protective helmets - Test methods - Part 8: Electrical properties

## 3 Definitions
### 3.1 industrial safety helmet
A helmet primarily designed to protect the upper part of a wearer's head against the effects of falling objects.

### 3.2 shell
The hard outer part of the helmet.

### 3.3 harness
The part of the helmet that fits onto the wearer's head and holds the helmet in place.

### 3.4 retention system
The complete assembly by means of which the helmet is retained on the head, including any straps, buckles, and adjustment devices.

### 3.5 comfort pad
A pad fitted to the inside of the helmet for the comfort of the wearer.

### 3.6 peak
A projection above the eye area of the helmet.

### 3.7 brim
A projection around the lower edge of the helmet.

## 4 Requirements

### 4.1 Construction
#### 4.1.1 Materials
The materials used shall be suitable for the intended use and shall not be adversely affected by exposure to ultraviolet radiation, extreme temperatures, or chemicals likely to be encountered in normal use.

#### 4.1.2 Finish
The helmet shall have a smooth finish without projections that could cause injury.

#### 4.1.3 Field of vision
The field of vision shall not be unduly restricted by the helmet.

### 4.2 Shock absorption
The helmet shall reduce the force transmitted to the headform when subjected to impact.

#### 4.2.1 Impact test on crown area
The force transmitted to the headform shall not exceed 5.0 kN when an impact is applied to the crown area.

#### 4.2.2 Impact test on front, rear, and side areas
The force transmitted to the headform shall not exceed 5.0 kN when an impact is applied to the front, rear, or side areas.

### 4.3 Resistance to penetration
The helmet shall prevent penetration by a sharp object.

A striker shall not make contact with the headform when dropped from a height of 1 m.

### 4.4 Flammability
The helmet shall not burn with a flame emission of more than 5 seconds after removal of the flame source.

### 4.5 Electrical properties
The helmet shall provide electrical insulation when tested in accordance with EN 13087-8.

### 4.6 Retention system
#### 4.6.1 Effectiveness
The retention system shall keep the helmet in place during normal use and under impact conditions.

#### 4.6.2 Strength
The retention system shall withstand a force of at least 150 N without breaking.

### 4.7 Marking
The helmet shall be marked with:
(a) The manufacturer's name or identifying mark;
(b) The year and quarter of manufacture;
(c) The type of helmet (EN 397);
(d) The size or size range;
(e) The shell material (if other than the standard material).

### 4.8 Optional requirements
#### 4.8.1 Very low temperature (-30°C or -40°C)
The helmet shall meet the shock absorption and penetration requirements after conditioning at very low temperatures.

#### 4.8.2 Molten metal splash
The helmet shall provide protection against molten metal splash.

#### 4.8.3 Lateral deformation
The helmet shall resist lateral deformation.

#### 4.8.4 Electrical insulation (1000 V AC or 1500 V DC)
The helmet shall provide enhanced electrical insulation.

## 5 Test methods

### 5.1 Conditioning
Helmets shall be conditioned at the specified temperature and humidity before testing.

### 5.2 Shock absorption test
The shock absorption shall be tested in accordance with EN 13087-2.

### 5.3 Penetration test
The penetration resistance shall be tested in accordance with EN 13087-3.

### 5.4 Flammability test
The flammability shall be tested in accordance with EN 13087-7.

### 5.5 Electrical properties test
The electrical properties shall be tested in accordance with EN 13087-8.

### 5.6 Retention system tests
The retention system shall be tested in accordance with EN 13087-4 and EN 13087-5.

## 6 Marking

### 6.1 Required marking
Each helmet shall be marked with:
(a) The manufacturer's name or trademark;
(b) The year and quarter of manufacture;
(c) 'EN 397';
(d) The size or size range.

### 6.2 Optional marking
Where applicable, the helmet shall also be marked with:
(a) '-30°C' or '-40°C' for very low temperature performance;
(b) 'MM' for molten metal splash protection;
(c) 'LD' for lateral deformation resistance;
(d) '440 V' for electrical insulation.

## 7 Information supplied by the manufacturer

### 7.1 User instructions
The manufacturer shall provide instructions containing:
(a) The intended use and limitations;
(b) Instructions for fitting and adjustment;
(c) Cleaning and maintenance instructions;
(d) Storage conditions;
(e) Inspection requirements;
(f) Replacement criteria;
(g) Warnings and precautions.

### 7.2 Packaging
The packaging shall be marked with:
(a) The manufacturer's name and address;
(b) The product identification;
(c) The standard number;
(d) The size;
(e) Any optional performance markings.

title: ANSI/ISEA Z89.1-2014 (R2019) - American National Standard for Industrial Head Protection
title_zh: ANSI/ISEA Z89.1-2014 (R2019) - 工业头部防护美国国家标准
summary: American national standard establishing requirements for industrial protective helmets (hard hats) to protect workers from impact and penetration hazards.
summary_zh: 美国国家标准，规定了工业防护头盔（安全帽）的要求，以保护工人免受冲击和穿刺危害。
full_text:
## 1. Scope and purpose

### 1.1 Scope
This standard establishes minimum performance requirements for industrial protective helmets (also called hard hats) to reduce the force of impact from falling objects and to protect against electrical shock.

### 1.2 Purpose
The purpose of this standard is to provide requirements for the design, performance, testing, and marking of protective helmets.

## 2. Definitions

### 2.1 protective helmet (hard hat)
A device designed to provide protection to the head against falling objects and electrical hazards.

### 2.2 shell
The rigid outer part of the helmet.

### 2.3 suspension
The assembly that fits onto the wearer's head and holds the shell away from the head.

### 2.4 crown straps
The straps that form the cradle of the suspension.

### 2.5 impact energy attenuation
The ability of the helmet to reduce the force of impact transmitted to the wearer.

### 2.6 penetration resistance
The ability of the helmet to prevent penetration by a pointed object.

## 3. Classification

### 3.1 Type I
Helmets designed to reduce the force of impact resulting from a blow only to the top of the head.

### 3.2 Type II
Helmets designed to reduce the force of impact resulting from a blow to the top or sides of the head.

### 3.3 Electrical classes
#### 3.3.1 Class G (General)
Helmets designed to reduce the danger of contact with low-voltage conductors. Tested at 2,200 volts (phase to ground).

#### 3.3.2 Class E (Electrical)
Helmets designed to reduce the danger of contact with higher voltage conductors. Tested at 20,000 volts (phase to ground).

#### 3.3.3 Class C (Conductive)
Helmets designed to provide impact and penetration resistance only. Not intended to provide protection against electrical hazards.

## 4. Performance requirements

### 4.1 Impact attenuation
#### 4.1.1 Type I helmets
The force transmitted to the headform shall not exceed 4450 N (1000 lbf) when an impact is delivered to the crown area.

#### 4.1.2 Type II helmets
The force transmitted to the headform shall not exceed 4450 N (1000 lbf) when an impact is delivered to the crown, front, rear, or side areas.

### 4.2 Penetration resistance
A test striker shall not make contact with the headform when dropped from a height of 1 m (39.4 inches).

### 4.3 Electrical insulation
#### 4.3.1 Class G helmets
The leakage current shall not exceed 3.0 mA when tested at 2,200 volts (phase to ground).

#### 4.3.2 Class E helmets
The leakage current shall not exceed 9.0 mA when tested at 20,000 volts (phase to ground), and shall not exceed 3.0 mA when tested at 2,200 volts (phase to ground).

### 4.4 Flammability resistance
The helmet shall not ignite when exposed to a flame for 5 seconds, and any flame shall self-extinguish within 5 seconds after removal of the flame source.

### 4.5 Force distribution (Type II only)
The force transmitted to the headform from an off-center impact shall be distributed over a larger area than for Type I helmets.

## 5. Test methods

### 5.1 Impact attenuation test
#### 5.1.1 Apparatus
A guided free-fall impact apparatus with a hemispherical striker of mass 5 kg (11 lb).

#### 5.1.2 Procedure
The helmet is placed on a standard headform. The striker is dropped from a height that produces an impact velocity of 5.2 m/s (17.1 ft/s).

#### 5.1.3 Acceptance criteria
The peak force transmitted to the headform shall not exceed 4450 N (1000 lbf).

### 5.2 Penetration test
#### 5.2.1 Apparatus
A guided free-fall penetration apparatus with a pointed striker of mass 1 kg (2.2 lb).

#### 5.2.2 Procedure
The striker is dropped from a height of 1 m (39.4 inches) onto the helmet mounted on a headform.

#### 5.2.3 Acceptance criteria
The striker shall not make contact with the headform.

### 5.3 Electrical insulation test
#### 5.3.1 Apparatus
An electrical test apparatus capable of applying the required test voltages.

#### 5.3.2 Procedure
The helmet is filled with water and electrodes are placed inside and outside the helmet. The specified test voltage is applied.

#### 5.3.3 Acceptance criteria
The leakage current shall not exceed the specified limits.

### 5.4 Flammability test
The helmet is exposed to a flame for 5 seconds. The helmet shall not continue to burn for more than 5 seconds after removal of the flame.

## 6. Marking and labeling

### 6.1 Shell marking
Each helmet shell shall be marked with:
(a) The manufacturer's name or identifying mark;
(b) The date of manufacture;
(c) The ANSI Z89.1 designation;
(d) The type and class designation (e.g., Type I, Class G);
(e) The size range.

### 6.2 Suspension marking
The suspension shall be marked with:
(a) The manufacturer's name or identifying mark;
(b) The model number;
(c) The date of manufacture.

### 6.3 Warning label
A warning label shall be affixed to the helmet stating:
(a) That the helmet is designed to provide limited protection;
(b) That the helmet should be inspected before each use;
(c) That the helmet should be replaced after impact.

## 7. Selection and use

### 7.1 Hazard assessment
Employers shall conduct a hazard assessment to determine the appropriate type and class of helmet.

### 7.2 Selection by hazard
#### 7.2.1 Impact hazards
For impact hazards from falling objects, select Type I or Type II helmets.

#### 7.2.2 Electrical hazards
For electrical hazards, select Class G or Class E helmets.

#### 7.2.3 Combined hazards
For combined impact and electrical hazards, select helmets that meet both requirements.

### 7.3 Inspection and maintenance
#### 7.3.1 Daily inspection
Helmets shall be inspected daily for cracks, dents, and other damage.

#### 7.3.2 Cleaning
Helmets shall be cleaned regularly with mild soap and water.

#### 7.3.3 Storage
Helmets shall be stored in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight.

### 7.4 Replacement
Helmets shall be replaced:
(a) After any impact;
(b) When visible damage is present;
(c) After 5 years from the date of manufacture (or as recommended by the manufacturer).

title: EN 352-1:2020 - Hearing protectors - Safety requirements and testing - Part 1: Ear-muffs
title_zh: EN 352-1:2020 - 听力防护器 - 安全要求和测试 - 第1部分：耳罩
summary: European standard specifying constructional, design, and performance requirements, marking requirements, and user information for ear-muffs.
summary_zh: 欧洲标准，规定了耳罩的结构、设计和性能要求、标识要求以及用户信息。
full_text:
## 1 Scope
This document specifies constructional, design and performance requirements, marking requirements and user information for ear-muffs.

This document does not apply to:
(a) Ear-muffs attached to a helmet or a face-shield;
(b) Ear-muffs for use in underwater activities;
(c) Ear-muffs for use in medical applications.

## 2 Normative references
- EN 352-2:2020, Hearing protectors - Safety requirements and testing - Part 2: Ear-plugs
- EN 352-3:2020, Hearing protectors - Safety requirements and testing - Part 3: Ear-muffs attached to a safety or industrial helmet
- EN 352-4:2020, Hearing protectors - Safety requirements and testing - Part 4: Level-dependent ear-muffs
- EN 352-5:2020, Hearing protectors - Safety requirements and testing - Part 5: Active noise reduction ear-muffs
- EN 352-6:2020, Hearing protectors - Safety requirements and testing - Part 6: Ear-muffs with safety-related audio input
- EN 352-7:2020, Hearing protectors - Safety requirements and testing - Part 7: Level-dependent ear-plugs
- EN 352-8:2020, Hearing protectors - Safety requirements and testing - Part 8: Entertainment audio ear-muffs
- EN ISO 4869-1:2018, Acoustics - Hearing protectors - Part 1: Subjective method for the measurement of sound attenuation

## 3 Terms and definitions
### 3.1 ear-muff
A hearing protector worn over the external ear, consisting of two ear-cups connected by a headband.

### 3.2 ear-cup
The part of the ear-muff that fits over the external ear and contains the sound-attenuating material.

### 3.3 cushion
The soft material that forms the seal between the ear-cup and the head.

### 3.4 headband
The part of the ear-muff that holds the ear-cups in position over the ears.

### 3.5 sound attenuation
The reduction in sound pressure level achieved by the hearing protector.

### 3.6 headband force
The force exerted by the headband to hold the ear-cups against the head.

## 4 Requirements

### 4.1 Construction and design
#### 4.1.1 Materials
The materials used shall be suitable for the intended use and shall not cause skin irritation or allergic reactions.

#### 4.1.2 Ear-cup rotation
The ear-cups shall be capable of rotating to fit the shape of the wearer's head.

#### 4.1.3 Headband adjustment
The headband shall be adjustable to fit a range of head sizes.

#### 4.1.4 Replaceable parts
Parts that are subject to wear (such as cushions and foam inserts) shall be replaceable.

### 4.2 Headband force
The headband force shall be between 10 N and 14 N when measured in accordance with the test method specified in this standard.

### 4.3 Cushion pressure
The pressure exerted by the cushion on the head shall not exceed 4500 Pa.

### 4.4 Resistance to damage when dropped
The ear-muff shall not be damaged when dropped from a height of 1.5 m onto a steel plate.

### 4.5 Resistance to damage at extreme temperatures
The ear-muff shall meet the sound attenuation requirements after conditioning at -20°C and +50°C.

### 4.6 Resistance to water immersion
The ear-muff shall meet the sound attenuation requirements after immersion in water.

### 4.7 Sound attenuation
The sound attenuation shall be measured in accordance with EN ISO 4869-1.

The assumed protection value (APV) shall be calculated and provided in the user information.

### 4.8 Electrical insulation
Where claimed, the ear-muff shall provide electrical insulation when tested in accordance with the specified method.

## 5 Test methods

### 5.1 Headband force test
The headband force is measured using a suitable gauge placed between the cushions.

### 5.2 Cushion pressure test
The cushion pressure is calculated from the headband force and the cushion area.

### 5.3 Drop test
The ear-muff is dropped from a height of 1.5 m onto a steel plate and then inspected for damage.

### 5.4 Temperature conditioning
The ear-muff is conditioned at -20°C and +50°C before testing for sound attenuation.

### 5.5 Water immersion test
The ear-muff is immersed in water for a specified period before testing for sound attenuation.

### 5.6 Sound attenuation test
The sound attenuation is measured using the subjective method specified in EN ISO 4869-1.

## 6 Marking

### 6.1 Required marking
Each ear-muff shall be marked with:
(a) The manufacturer's name or identifying mark;
(b) The model or type designation;
(c) The number of this standard (EN 352-1);
(d) The direction for fitting (left/right if applicable);
(e) The sound attenuation data (H, M, L values).

### 6.2 Optional marking
Where applicable, the ear-muff shall also be marked with:
(a) The CE marking;
(b) The Notified Body number;
(c) Electrical insulation marking.

## 7 User information

### 7.1 Required information
The manufacturer shall provide instructions containing:
(a) The intended use and limitations;
(b) Instructions for fitting and adjustment;
(c) The sound attenuation data (H, M, L values and APV);
(d) Instructions for checking the fit;
(e) Cleaning and maintenance instructions;
(f) Storage conditions;
(g) Warnings and precautions;
(h) Information on the expected service life.

### 7.2 Sound attenuation data
The manufacturer shall provide the following sound attenuation data:
(a) Mean attenuation values at standard frequencies;
(b) Standard deviation values;
(c) H, M, L values;
(d) Assumed protection values (APV) at standard frequencies.

### 7.3 Fitting instructions
The fitting instructions shall include:
(a) How to position the ear-cups over the ears;
(b) How to adjust the headband for a proper fit;
(c) How to check for proper seal.

title: ANSI S3.19-1974 (R2019) - American National Standard for the Measurement of Real-Ear Attenuation of Hearing Protectors
title_zh: ANSI S3.19-1974 (R2019) - 听力防护器实耳衰减测量美国国家标准
summary: American national standard specifying the real-ear method for measuring the attenuation of hearing protectors at threshold.
summary_zh: 美国国家标准，规定了在阈值处测量听力防护器衰减的实耳方法。
full_text:
## 1. Scope

This standard provides a real-ear method for measuring the attenuation of hearing protectors at threshold.

## 2. Definitions

### 2.1 hearing protector
Any device or material that can be worn to reduce the level of sound entering the ear.

### 2.2 real-ear attenuation at threshold (REAT)
The difference between the hearing threshold measured with the hearing protector in place and the hearing threshold measured without the hearing protector, expressed in decibels.

### 2.3 octave band
A frequency band whose upper limit is twice its lower limit.

### 2.4 one-third octave band
A frequency band whose upper limit is approximately 1.26 times its lower limit.

## 3. Test method

### 3.1 Subjects
A minimum of 10 subjects with normal hearing shall be used for the test.

### 3.2 Test environment
Testing shall be conducted in a sound field that meets the requirements for audiometric test rooms.

### 3.3 Test signals
The test signals shall be one-third octave bands of noise centered at the following frequencies:
- 125 Hz
- 250 Hz
- 500 Hz
- 1000 Hz
- 2000 Hz
- 3000 Hz
- 4000 Hz
- 6000 Hz
- 8000 Hz

### 3.4 Test procedure
#### 3.4.1 Open-ear thresholds
The hearing thresholds of each subject shall be measured without the hearing protector in place.

#### 3.4.2 Protected-ear thresholds
The hearing thresholds of each subject shall be measured with the hearing protector properly fitted.

#### 3.4.3 Attenuation calculation
The real-ear attenuation at threshold (REAT) shall be calculated as the difference between the open-ear threshold and the protected-ear threshold at each test frequency.

### 3.5 Statistical analysis
#### 3.5.1 Mean attenuation
The mean attenuation shall be calculated for each test frequency.

#### 3.5.2 Standard deviation
The standard deviation shall be calculated for each test frequency.

#### 3.5.3 Noise reduction rating (NRR)
The NRR shall be calculated using the following formula:

NRR = (Mean attenuation at 500 Hz + Mean attenuation at 1000 Hz + Mean attenuation at 2000 Hz) / 3 - 2 * (Standard deviation at 500 Hz + Standard deviation at 1000 Hz + Standard deviation at 2000 Hz) / 3 - 3 dB correction factor

## 4. Reporting

### 4.1 Required data
The test report shall include:
(a) The mean attenuation at each test frequency;
(b) The standard deviation at each test frequency;
(c) The number of subjects tested;
(d) The noise reduction rating (NRR);
(e) The test conditions.

### 4.2 Optional data
The test report may also include:
(a) Individual subject data;
(b) Statistical analysis;
(c) Comparison with other hearing protectors.

## 5. Labeling

### 5.1 Required labeling
Each hearing protector shall be labeled with:
(a) The manufacturer's name or trademark;
(b) The model or type designation;
(c) The noise reduction rating (NRR);
(d) Instructions for proper fitting and use.

### 5.2 NRR display
The NRR shall be prominently displayed on the packaging and in the user instructions.

## 6. Use of NRR

### 6.1 Estimating protected noise exposure
The protected noise exposure level can be estimated by subtracting the NRR from the measured noise level.

### 6.2 Derating
The NRR may be derated by 50% for use in real-world conditions where proper fitting cannot be ensured.

### 6.3 Limitations
The NRR is based on laboratory testing and may not reflect the actual protection achieved in the workplace.

title: GB 14866-2006 - Personal eye-protectors
issuing_authority: General Administration of Quality Supervision, Inspection and Quarantine
summary: Chinese national standard specifying technical requirements, test methods and marking for personal eye-protectors used in various occupational applications.
summary_zh: 中国国家标准，规定了各种职业应用中个人用眼护具的技术要求、测试方法和标识。
full_text:
## 1 范围
本标准规定了个人用眼护具的技术要求、试验方法、检验规则及标志、包装、运输、贮存。

本标准适用于除核辐射、X射线、激光、紫外线、红外线及其他辐射以外的各类个人用眼护具。

## 2 规范性引用文件
- GB/T 191 包装储运图示标志
- GB/T 2410 透明塑料透光率和雾度试验方法
- GB/T 2423.17 电工电子产品基本环境试验规程 试验Ka：盐雾试验方法
- GB/T 2423.22 电工电子产品基本环境试验规程 试验N：温度变化试验方法

## 3 术语和定义
### 3.1 眼护具
用于保护眼睛免受各种伤害的防护器具。

### 3.2 镜片
眼护具上供佩戴者观察的透明或半透明部件。

### 3.3 镜框
用于固定镜片的框架部件。

### 3.4 侧面防护
用于保护眼睛侧面的防护部件。

### 3.5 防冲击眼护具
用于防护固体异物冲击眼睛的眼护具。

### 3.6 防化学液眼护具
用于防护化学液体飞溅伤害眼睛的眼护具。

### 3.7 防热眼护具
用于防护高温伤害眼睛的眼护具。

### 3.8 防辐射眼护具
用于防护各种辐射伤害眼睛的眼护具。

## 4 分类

### 4.1 按用途分类
(a) 防冲击眼护具；
(b) 防化学液眼护具；
(c) 防热眼护具；
(d) 防辐射眼护具。

### 4.2 按结构分类
(a) 眼镜式；
(b) 护目罩式；
(c) 面罩式。

## 5 技术要求

### 5.1 一般要求
#### 5.1.1 材料
眼护具所使用的材料应满足以下要求：
(a) 不得使用会引起皮肤过敏的材料；
(b) 镜片材料应具有良好的光学性能；
(c) 框架材料应具有足够的强度。

#### 5.1.2 结构
(a) 眼护具应结构牢固，易于佩戴和摘脱；
(b) 可调节部件应调节方便、可靠；
(c) 与面部接触的部分应光滑，无锐边。

### 5.2 光学性能
#### 5.2.1 屈光度
镜片的屈光度偏差不应超过 ±0.09D。

#### 5.2.2 棱镜度
镜片的几何中心水平棱镜度互差不应超过 0.25Δ。

#### 5.2.3 透光率
无色镜片的可见光透光率不应小于 89%。

#### 5.2.4 雾度
镜片的雾度不应超过 2%。

### 5.3 机械性能
#### 5.3.1 抗冲击性能
(a) 一级抗冲击：承受直径22mm、重45g钢球从1.3m高度自由落下的冲击；
(b) 二级抗冲击：承受直径16mm、重16g钢球从1.3m高度自由落下的冲击。

#### 5.3.2 耐磨损性能
镜片经磨损试验后，透光率下降不应超过 5%。

#### 5.3.3 耐热性能
眼护具经高温试验后，应无变形、无裂纹、无脱落。

### 5.4 化学性能
#### 5.4.1 耐腐蚀性能
金属部件经盐雾试验后，应无明显腐蚀。

#### 5.4.2 耐化学液性能
防化学液眼护具经化学液试验后，应无变形、无裂纹、无脱落。

### 5.5 阻燃性能
眼护具经阻燃试验后，续燃时间不应超过 5s。

## 6 试验方法

### 6.1 光学性能试验
按GB/T 2410规定的方法进行透光率和雾度试验。

### 6.2 抗冲击性能试验
将眼护具固定在头模上，按规定的冲击条件进行试验。

### 6.3 耐热性能试验
将眼护具置于 (55 ± 2)°C 的环境中保持 2h，然后检查外观。

### 6.4 盐雾试验
按GB/T 2423.17规定的方法进行盐雾试验。

### 6.5 阻燃试验
将眼护具暴露于火焰中 5s，然后测量续燃时间。

## 7 检验规则

### 7.1 出厂检验
每批产品应进行出厂检验，检验项目包括：
(a) 外观检查；
(b) 尺寸检查；
(c) 光学性能检查。

### 7.2 型式检验
有下列情况之一时，应进行型式检验：
(a) 新产品试制鉴定；
(b) 正式生产后，如结构、材料、工艺有较大改变；
(c) 停产一年以上恢复生产；
(d) 出厂检验结果与上次型式检验有较大差异。

## 8 标志、包装、运输、贮存

### 8.1 标志
每个眼护具应标明：
(a) 制造厂名或商标；
(b) 产品型号；
(c) 执行标准编号；
(d) 生产日期或批号。

### 8.2 包装
包装应能防止产品在运输和贮存过程中受到损坏。

### 8.3 运输
运输过程中应避免重压、雨淋和日晒。

### 8.4 贮存
产品应贮存在干燥、通风的库房内，远离热源和腐蚀性物质。

title: GB 2811-2019 - Safety helmets
summary: Chinese national standard specifying technical requirements, test methods, inspection rules and marking for safety helmets, replacing GB 2811-2007.
summary_zh: 中国国家标准，规定了安全帽的技术要求、测试方法、检验规则和标识，替代GB 2811-2007。
full_text:
## 1 范围
本标准规定了安全帽的分类、标记、技术要求、检验规则及标识。

本标准适用于作业场所头部防护用的安全帽。

本标准不适用于消防、应急救援、运动用头部防护装备。

## 2 规范性引用文件
- GB/T 191 包装储运图示标志
- GB/T 2812 安全帽测试方法
- GB/T 5725 安全网

## 3 术语和定义
### 3.1 安全帽
对人体头部受坠落物及其他特定因素引起的伤害起防护作用的帽子。

### 3.2 帽壳
安全帽外表面组成部分，由帽舌、帽檐和顶筋组成。

### 3.3 帽衬
帽壳内部部件的总称，由帽箍、吸汗带、缓冲垫、衬带等组成。

### 3.4 帽箍
绕头围起固定作用的带圈。

### 3.5 吸汗带
帽箍上与额头直接接触的部分。

### 3.6 下颏带
系在下巴上，起辅助固定作用的带子。

### 3.7 水平间距
安全帽在佩戴时，帽箍与帽壳内侧之间在水平面上的径向距离。

### 3.8 垂直间距
安全帽在佩戴时，头顶最高点与帽壳内表面之间的轴向距离（不包括顶筋）。

## 4 分类

### 4.1 按用途分类
(a) 一般作业类（Y类）；
(b) 特殊作业类（T类）。

### 4.2 按性能分类
(a) 普通型；
(b) 阻燃型；
(c) 侧向刚性型；
(d) 耐低温型；
(e) 耐极高温型；
(f) 电绝缘型；
(g) 防静电型。

## 5 技术要求

### 5.1 一般要求
#### 5.1.1 材料
安全帽所使用的材料应满足以下要求：
(a) 不得使用有毒、有害或引起皮肤过敏的材料；
(b) 不得使用回收、再生材料作为安全帽主体材料；
(c) 材料应具有足够的强度，在正常使用寿命中不应出现破损或变形。

#### 5.1.2 结构
(a) 帽壳应制成光滑流线型，表面不应有裂纹、气泡、缺损及其他有损性能的缺陷；
(b) 帽衬组件应牢固地安装在帽壳上，正常使用中不应松脱；
(c) 下颏带应采用无毒、阻燃材料制成。

### 5.2 尺寸要求
#### 5.2.1 帽壳内部尺寸
长：195mm ~ 250mm；宽：170mm ~ 220mm；高：120mm ~ 150mm。

#### 5.2.2 水平间距
5mm ~ 20mm。

#### 5.2.3 垂直间距
25mm ~ 50mm。

#### 5.2.4 佩戴高度
80mm ~ 90mm。

### 5.3 性能要求
#### 5.3.1 冲击吸收性能
经高温、低温、浸水预处理后做冲击测试，传递到头模的力不应大于 4900N，帽壳不得有碎片脱落。

#### 5.3.2 耐穿刺性能
经高温、低温、浸水预处理后做穿刺测试，钢锥不得接触头模表面，帽壳不得有碎片脱落。

#### 5.3.3 阻燃性能
续燃时间不应超过 5s，帽壳不得烧穿。

#### 5.3.4 侧向刚性
最大变形不应大于 40mm，残余变形不应大于 15mm。

#### 5.3.5 耐低温性能
经低温预处理后，冲击吸收性能和耐穿刺性能应符合5.3.1和5.3.2的要求。

#### 5.3.6 耐极高温性能
经极高温预处理后，冲击吸收性能和耐穿刺性能应符合5.3.1和5.3.2的要求。

#### 5.3.7 电绝缘性能
泄漏电流不应超过 1.2mA。

#### 5.3.8 防静电性能
表面电阻率不应大于 1×10⁹Ω。

#### 5.3.9 耐熔融金属飞溅性能
经熔融金属飞溅试验后，损坏区域不应大于 10mm²。

### 5.4 下颏带强度
下颏带发生破坏时的力值应在 150N ~ 250N 之间。

## 6 试验方法

### 6.1 冲击吸收性能试验
按GB/T 2812规定的方法进行试验。

### 6.2 耐穿刺性能试验
按GB/T 2812规定的方法进行试验。

### 6.3 阻燃性能试验
按GB/T 2812规定的方法进行试验。

### 6.4 侧向刚性试验
按GB/T 2812规定的方法进行试验。

### 6.5 电绝缘性能试验
按GB/T 2812规定的方法进行试验。

## 7 检验规则

### 7.1 出厂检验
每批产品应进行出厂检验，检验项目包括：
(a) 外观检查；
(b) 尺寸检查；
(c) 冲击吸收性能；
(d) 耐穿刺性能。

### 7.2 型式检验
有下列情况之一时，应进行型式检验：
(a) 新产品试制鉴定；
(b) 正式生产后，如结构、材料、工艺有较大改变；
(c) 停产一年以上恢复生产；
(d) 出厂检验结果与上次型式检验有较大差异。

## 8 标识

### 8.1 永久标识
每顶安全帽应有以下永久标识：
(a) 本标准编号（GB 2811-2019）；
(b) 制造厂名、商标；
(c) 生产日期（年、月）；
(d) 产品名称（由生产厂命名）；
(e) 产品的分类标记；
(f) 产品的强制报废期限。

### 8.2 产品说明
每个安全帽均要附加产品说明，说明内容包括：
(a) 安全帽的适用场所；
(b) 安全帽的正确佩戴方法；
(c) 安全帽的维护、保养方法；
(d) 安全帽的判废条件；
(e) 贮存条件。

## 9 包装、运输和贮存

### 9.1 包装
产品应有合适的包装，包装应能防止在运输和贮存过程中受到损坏。

### 9.2 运输
运输过程中应避免重压、日晒和雨淋。

### 9.3 贮存
安全帽应贮存在干燥、通风的仓库内，远离热源和腐蚀性物质。

title: EN 136:1998 - Respiratory protective devices - Full face masks - Requirements, testing, marking
title_zh: EN 136:1998 - 呼吸防护装置 - 全面罩 - 要求、测试、标记
summary: European standard specifying the minimum requirements for full face masks for respiratory protection.
summary_zh: 欧洲标准，规定了呼吸防护用全面罩的最低要求。
full_text:
## 1 Scope
This European Standard specifies the minimum requirements for full face masks for respiratory protection.

This standard does not apply to respiratory protective devices used for underwater activities or for medical purposes.

## 2 Normative references
- EN 132:1998, Respiratory protective devices - Definitions of terms and pictograms
- EN 134:2002, Respiratory protective devices - Nomenclature of components
- EN 143:2000, Respiratory protective devices - Particle filters

## 3 Definitions
### 3.1 full face mask
A respiratory protective device that covers the eyes, nose, mouth, and chin and is held in position by a head harness.

### 3.2 face blank
The part of the full face mask that forms a seal with the wearer's face.

### 3.3 visor
The transparent part of the full face mask through which the wearer sees.

### 3.4 speech diaphragm
A device fitted to the full face mask to facilitate speech transmission.

### 3.5 exhalation valve
A valve that allows exhaled air to leave the facepiece.

## 4 Classification

### 4.1 Class 1
Full face masks designed for light duty and normal use.

### 4.2 Class 2
Full face masks designed for heavy duty and more demanding use.

### 4.3 Class 3
Full face masks designed for firefighting and other emergency services.

## 5 Requirements

### 5.1 Materials
The materials used shall be suitable for the intended use and shall not cause skin irritation or allergic reactions.

### 5.2 Construction
#### 5.2.1 Face blank
The face blank shall be designed to fit a range of face sizes and shapes.

#### 5.2.2 Visor
The visor shall provide adequate field of vision and shall be resistant to impact and scratching.

#### 5.2.3 Head harness
The head harness shall hold the face mask securely in position and shall be adjustable.

#### 5.2.4 Valves
The inhalation and exhalation valves shall function correctly and shall be easily replaceable.

### 5.3 Performance requirements
#### 5.3.1 Leakage
The total inward leakage shall not exceed 0.05% when tested in accordance with the specified method.

#### 5.3.2 Breathing resistance
The inhalation resistance shall not exceed 3.0 mbar at 30 l/min.
The exhalation resistance shall not exceed 3.0 mbar at 160 l/min.

#### 5.3.3 Field of vision
The field of vision shall not be less than 70% of the natural field of vision.

#### 5.3.4 Speech transmission
The speech transmission index shall not be less than 0.50.

#### 5.3.5 Impact resistance
The visor shall withstand impact from a 6 mm steel ball traveling at 120 m/s.

### 5.4 Comfort and practicality
The full face mask shall be comfortable to wear and shall not cause undue fatigue during normal use.

## 6 Test methods

### 6.1 Leakage test
The total inward leakage shall be measured using human test subjects performing specified exercises.

### 6.2 Breathing resistance test
The breathing resistance shall be measured using a breathing machine or human test subjects.

### 6.3 Field of vision test
The field of vision shall be measured using a perimeter or other suitable device.

### 6.4 Speech transmission test
The speech transmission index shall be measured using the method specified in the standard.

### 6.5 Impact resistance test
The impact resistance of the visor shall be tested using a steel ball fired at the specified velocity.

## 7 Marking

### 7.1 Required marking
Each full face mask shall be marked with:
(a) The manufacturer's name or identifying mark;
(b) The model or type designation;
(c) The class (1, 2, or 3);
(d) The number of this standard (EN 136);
(e) The year of manufacture.

### 7.2 Additional marking
Where applicable, the full face mask shall also be marked with:
(a) The CE marking;
(b) The Notified Body number;
(c) The filter type and class.

## 8 Information supplied by the manufacturer

### 8.1 User instructions
The manufacturer shall provide instructions containing:
(a) The intended use and limitations;
(b) Instructions for fitting and adjustment;
(c) Instructions for checking the seal;
(d) Cleaning and disinfection instructions;
(e) Storage conditions;
(f) Warnings and precautions;
(g) Information on the expected service life.

### 8.2 Packaging
The packaging shall be marked with:
(a) The manufacturer's name and address;
(b) The product identification;
(c) The standard number;
(d) The class;
(e) The size or size range.
